mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity deleveraging that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in housing construction and consumer credit expansion indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during concurrent HOUST and RSAFS accelerations.

mechanism_narrative: A plateau or decline in industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with flat yield curve
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, directly supporting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over a weekly horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 acceleration, or the rolling Rank IC remains negative for more than 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer spending, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 65% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 2.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of quantitative easing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll growth combined with stable consumer price inflation signals a healthy wage-price dynamic that supports corporate pricing power without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL signal fails to generate a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically flat when payroll growth exceeds 150k monthly and CPI remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in revolving consumer credit indicates household balance sheet stress and reduced discretionary spending, which depresses retail and service sector earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances following a 2% monthly RSAFS drop, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 2-year walk-forward test.
